project_structure: Monorepo with three workspace packages: packages/cli (citty-based CLI binary `fab`, owns templates/bootstrap/ + templates/agents-md/ + skills + hooks; install.ts is the only writer of repo-root anchor files), packages/server (MCP server + doctor service; pure logic, no template assets in dist), packages/shared (Zod schemas + i18n + atomic-write helpers). Doctor is a server-side service (packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts, 4603 lines, 30+ inspection helpers) exported via @fenglimg/fabric-server and invoked from CLI command at packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts. Three entrypoints exist on the report side: runDoctorReport (read-only), runDoctorFix (fixable_error mutations), runDoctorApplyLint (knowledge-frontmatter mutations, behind --fix-knowledge). Each inspection follows a consistent inspectX → createXCheck → fixX trio pattern. The repo-root bootstrap surface today is just AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md) at the project root, plus the HTML-comment-wrapped <!-- fabric:knowledge-base:* --> managed block written into CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules by `addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection`. There is no .fabric/AGENTS.md or .fabric/project-rules.md today — rc.19 introduces those as the canonical L1 source and the L2 concat input respectively.

relevant_files.rationale: Sole owner of the doctor pipeline. Houses runDoctorReport (L704), runDoctorFix (L893), runDoctorApplyLint (L1024), the inspectBootstrapAnchor (L1779) / createBootstrapAnchorCheck (L1786) pair that today only checks file existence (not template parity), the check-aggregation chain (L784-857) where new L1/L2 inspections must be appended, and the --fix dispatcher chain (L898-975) where one-time marker migration would land. ALL new rc.19 logic — two-layer drift detection + marker-rename migration — anchors here.

key_code.description: Top-level reporter. Builds inspections in Promise.all (L708-L718) + synchronous block (L719-L783), assembles checks[] array (L784-L857), reduces statuses. NEW L1 + L2 inspections must be inserted into the inspection block AND createXCheck calls appended to checks[]. Status reduction (reduceStatus) auto-handles error roll-up so a new fixable_error check immediately changes report.status to error.

Sequential dispatcher: each fixable_error code gets one if-block that calls a fix helper, then pushes findIssue(...) into `fixed`. Pattern to mirror for the marker migration: `if (before.fixable_errors.some((issue) => issue.code === 'bootstrap_marker_migration_required')) { await migrateKnowledgeBaseMarker(projectRoot); fixed.push(...); }`. Order matters — reconcileKnowledge (L935) is the heaviest mutation; marker migration is a simple text rewrite and should run BEFORE reconcile to avoid double-pass.

Current bootstrap check is existence-only: BootstrapAnchorInspection = { hasAgentsMd, hasClaudeMd }; missing both → fixable_error code 'bootstrap_anchor_missing' with actionHint 'Run fabric install'. rc.19 EXTENDS this surface: keep existing existence semantics, ADD two sibling inspections (inspectL1BootstrapDrift, inspectL2ConcatDrift) that produce their own fixable_error codes. Code clean-slate prefers two new inspections rather than overloading BootstrapAnchorInspection — keeps blast radius narrow and makes per-check kill switches trivial.

Constructor pattern for new checks. issueCheck(name, status, kind, code, message, actionHint) — kind='fixable_error' surfaces in report.fixable_errors and runDoctorFix routes by code. Set actionHint to the literal 'Run `fab doctor --fix` to ...' string the rest of the file uses so output stays consistent.

Closest pattern reference for a one-time migration fix that rewrites a file via atomicWriteJson/atomicWriteText. Idempotent: re-running --fix after migration finds nothing to do because the old marker is gone. Marker rename should mirror this shape: read file → s/fabric:knowledge-base/fabric:bootstrap/ on the two marker tokens → atomicWriteText. Emits no ledger event today; rc.19 marker migration SHOULD emit one (see integration_points below).

The set of files doctor summary tracks for existence. Currently lists .fabric/forensic.json, agents.meta.json, fabric-config.json, events.jsonl, knowledge. rc.19 adds .fabric/AGENTS.md and .fabric/project-rules.md so summary.targetFiles surfaces their presence in --json output.

relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration targets this file because it owns 100% of the surface where rc.19's two-layer drift detection + marker rename migration land. Every new inspection function, every new fixable_error code, the runDoctorFix branch, and the doctor summary expansion all live here.

relevant_files.rationale: 3519-line companion test file. Existing patterns 'mcp_config_in_wrong_file --fix removes mcpServers.fabric ... and writes ledger event' (L202-L231) and the bootstrap_anchor_missing tests (L737-L795) are the direct templates for new tests: build a fixture project root with deliberately-drifted .fabric/AGENTS.md vs canonical template, assert report flags fixable_error, assert --fix restores byte-equality, assert idempotent re-run produces no further mutation. Marker migration test mirrors mcp_config_migrated event assertion (events.map).

key_code.description: Existing three-test pattern: passes when AGENTS.md OR CLAUDE.md exists, fixable_error when neither exists. rc.19 adds parallel L1/L2 drift triplets: (a) ok when .fabric/AGENTS.md byte-matches canonical template, (b) fixable_error when bytes differ, (c) --fix overwrites and re-run is clean. Plus a CRLF-no-normalization assertion: write CRLF on disk and verify the check STILL fires drift (we never silently normalize).

Closest existing pattern for 'doctor --fix rewrites a file AND emits a ledger event of a specific event_type'. The test reads events.jsonl post-fix and asserts the event_type token is present. Marker migration test should follow this shape — add a new event_type 'bootstrap_marker_migrated' to event-ledger.ts schema and assert it appears in the ledger after --fix.

relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration targets test patterns to mirror — existing bootstrap_anchor_missing and mcp_config_in_wrong_file --fix triplets give exact assertion templates for rc.19's L1/L2 drift + one-time marker rename tests.

relevant_files.rationale: Sole owner of the managed-block contract: defines FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN_MARKER / FABRIC_SECTION_END_MARKER (L235-L236), FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX (L245-L246), buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection (L291-L304), and addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection (L638-L708) which writes the block into all three SECTION_TARGETS (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules). rc.19's L2 downstream drift compares this exact block (post-rename to fabric:bootstrap:begin/end) byte-for-byte against the concat of .fabric/AGENTS.md + .fabric/project-rules.md. ALL three marker constants and the regex need rename pre-flight, and a const for the new bootstrap content builder must be exposed so doctor (server-side) can read the canonical block without depending on cli internals.

key_code.description: The two literal strings '<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:begin -->' / '<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:end -->' that rc.19 renames to 'fabric:bootstrap:begin' / 'fabric:bootstrap:end'. Search-and-replace must hit these two constants, FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX (L245-L246), the JSDoc references at L227-L233 and L617-L619, AND the two test fixture constants in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts (L442-L443) and uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts (L329-L330). Doctor migration logic must recognize BOTH old + new markers during the migration window.

Idempotent in-place replace writer for the managed block across SECTION_TARGETS. Uses atomicWriteText and FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX to either replace-in-place or append. Post-rc.19 the block CONTENT becomes the concat of .fabric/AGENTS.md + .fabric/project-rules.md (or a derived render), and the markers become fabric:bootstrap:*. This is where install writes the L2-managed text that doctor must compare against.

relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration must understand the install-side managed-block contract because L2 downstream drift detection compares the on-disk managed block (written by this file) against the .fabric/AGENTS.md + project-rules.md concat. The byte-level diff direction is install-output ↔ canonical-source, and any CRLF asymmetry here is the 'install-side bug' the task spec calls out.

relevant_files.rationale: One of the three canonical Fabric bootstrap template files shipped with the CLI npm package (templates/bootstrap/ directory). Per task spec, rc.19's L1 drift compares 'Fabric package canonical bootstrap template' ↔ `.fabric/AGENTS.md`. This file + AGENTS.md.template (templates/agents-md/) are the canonical sources. Critical design question: server-side doctor has no direct dep on the CLI package, so it cannot import these via require/path resolution out of the box — see clarification_needs.
relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration flags this file because it represents the L1 'upstream truth' input to the new drift check, but its physical location inside packages/cli/templates/ creates a cross-package access constraint for the server-side doctor logic.

relevant_files.rationale: The other canonical bootstrap template (AGENTS.md anchor). Contains {projectName} / {frameworkKind} interpolation placeholders, meaning rc.19's L1 drift check cannot do a naive byte-compare — it must render placeholders to expected values first, then byte-compare the rendered result against .fabric/AGENTS.md. This adds a render-then-compare wrinkle the task spec does not explicitly cover.

key_code.description: Contains '{ projectName }' (L1) and '{ frameworkKind }' (L19) interpolation tokens. Doctor's L1 drift detection must either (a) substitute the tokens with current project values before byte-compare, OR (b) compare with token placeholders preserved (treating .fabric/AGENTS.md as the source of truth that retains tokens). Choice depends on whether rc.19 installs the template verbatim or post-interpolation.

relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration surfaces this file because L1 drift detection's byte-compare needs an upstream canonical that is well-defined — the interpolation tokens force a clarification on render-mode vs verbatim-mode.

relevant_files.rationale: CLI-side wrapper that wires --fix flag (L76-L80) to runDoctorFix(target). The marker migration is a fix mutation so it lands behind the existing --fix branch (L186-L188); no new CLI flag needed. The existing --fix-knowledge / --fix mutual exclusion (L137-L141) already gates concurrent surfaces. The emitDoctorRunEventBestEffort hook (L301-L316) shows the best-effort observability pattern marker migration should mirror.

key_code.description: Today: `} else if (fix) { fixReport = await runDoctorFix(resolution.target); report = fixReport.report; }`. No change needed at the CLI surface — server-side runDoctorFix dispatching the new fixable_error code is the only wiring required. The --fix flag plumbing terminates entirely server-side.

Existing best-effort emission of doctor_run event with mode + issues count after every doctor invocation. rc.19 marker migration should NOT emit through this path (it's a separate one-shot event) but should follow the same best-effort/try-silent failure mode.

relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration confirms the --fix flag is already plumbed correctly; rc.19 needs zero new CLI args or commands — pure server-side surface extension.

relevant_files.rationale: Zod schema declaring the discriminated union of all events.jsonl event_type literals (event_type: z.literal(...) at L16, L30, L44, L55, L72, L91, L99, L107 (event_ledger_truncated), L115 (mcp_config_migrated), L122, L130, L139, L313 (doctor_run), etc.). rc.19 must add a new event_type literal — recommended name: 'bootstrap_marker_migrated' modeled after 'mcp_config_migrated' (L115). Schema-first means tests can assert via the typed shape rather than free-form string match.

key_code.description: Direct template for the new event type. event_type: z.literal('mcp_config_migrated') plus the migration metadata fields. The marker migration event should record the path migrated, the byte count of the rewrite, and a count of marker occurrences replaced (for audit). One event per project root, emitted once.

relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration identifies this as the schema gate for the new ledger event emitted by the one-time marker migration under --fix.

relevant_files.rationale: Owner of appendEventLedgerEvent + truncateLedgerToLastNewline. Already used by runDoctorFix for event_ledger_truncated (L963-L968). The marker migration fix path must append a bootstrap_marker_migrated event via this same helper. Atomic-write semantics + partial-write recovery (truncateLedgerToLastNewline) already handle the failure modes.
relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration confirms the event emission helper for the marker-rename audit trail is already in place and used adjacently by event_ledger_partial_write fix.

relevant_files.rationale: Provides atomicWriteText (already imported at doctor.ts L24). The marker rename + L1 fix both use this for the rewrite path. Pattern reference: atomic-write avoids the half-written-file failure mode the task spec implicitly assumes for byte-level diff stability.
relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration identifies the canonical atomic-write primitive that both the L1 --fix overwrite and the marker migration --fix must use to satisfy the 'byte-level' contract without half-written-file risk.

relevant_files.rationale: Holds the SECTION_BEGIN / SECTION_END literal fixtures at L442-L443 — must be renamed in lockstep with the source constants. Confirms the bidirectional contract of the marker rename: install writes new markers, doctor recognizes both old and new during migration window, uninstall (uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L329-L330) cleans new markers.
relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration tracks every existing fixture using the old marker string — these need rename to fabric:bootstrap:* in lockstep with the migration to keep the install test contract green.

relevant_files.rationale: Uninstall side of the managed block contract. SECTION_BEGIN_UN / SECTION_END_UN at L329-L330. Uninstall must strip the NEW marker name post-rename; doctor migration handles the in-place upgrade for existing installs.
relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration confirms uninstall stripping must use the new marker token; cross-checks that the rename is consistent across install/doctor/uninstall surfaces.

relevant_files.rationale: Imports SECTION_TARGETS and FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX from skills-and-hooks.ts (L14) and strips the managed block during uninstall (L276-L299). Marker rename naturally propagates here via the shared imports; no separate rename needed, but the JSDoc at L278 references the literal marker string and must be updated.
relevant_files.topic_relation: doctor-flow exploration flags this file as transitively affected by the marker constant rename — confirms the rename is a single source-of-truth change rather than scattered string literals.

patterns: Inspection trio convention (inspectX → createXCheck → fixX or applyX) — every check follows this shape. createXCheck functions return DoctorCheck via issueCheck(name, status, kind, code, message, actionHint) on failure or okCheck(name, message) on success. Fixable errors must have kind='fixable_error' AND a matching if-block inside runDoctorFix that dispatches by `issue.code`. ActionHint format is uniformly 'Run `fab doctor --fix` to ...' so users get a self-bootstrapping next step. Atomic-write rewrites use atomicWriteText / atomicWriteJson — never raw fs.writeFile (half-written-file mitigation). Ledger event emission for mutation fixes uses appendEventLedgerEvent with a typed event_type literal added to event-ledger.ts schema; tests assert via events.map((e) => e.event_type).toContain(...). The managed-block contract is single-source-of-truth in packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts — markers, regex, builder, writer all live in one file and uninstall + tests re-import the constants. Idempotency invariant: every fix must produce 0 mutations on second --fix invocation on the same state (asserted explicitly in mcp_config_in_wrong_file and counter_desync tests).
dependencies: doctor.ts internal: contextCache (cache.js), _shared.ts (getEventLedgerPath/sha256/ensureParentDirectory), event-ledger.ts (appendEventLedgerEvent/readEventLedger/truncateLedgerToLastNewline), knowledge-meta-builder.ts (buildKnowledgeMeta/writeKnowledgeMeta), knowledge-sync.ts (reconcileKnowledge). doctor.ts external: @fenglimg/fabric-shared (schemas + types), @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node (detectFramework + atomic-write). CRITICAL CROSS-PACKAGE GAP: packages/server has NO dependency on packages/cli, so server-side doctor cannot directly require() or readTemplate() from packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/. rc.19 needs a design decision: (a) lift canonical templates to packages/shared with re-export so both CLI install and server doctor consume from one place, (b) keep templates in CLI and bundle a hash/copy into shared at build time, (c) move just the canonical bootstrap string constants into shared, leave file-on-disk templates in CLI for human-readable reference. Option (a) is cleanest given pre-user clean-slate preference. Marker constants today live in CLI (skills-and-hooks.ts) — same problem; doctor's L2 check needs access to FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN_MARKER / END_MARKER (post-rename to fabric:bootstrap:*) without importing CLI. Move the marker constants and buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection (or its bootstrap successor) into shared as part of rc.19 prep.
integration_points: 1) runDoctorReport check assembly (doctor.ts L784-L857) — append two new createXCheck calls: createL1BootstrapDriftCheck + createL2ConcatDriftCheck. Add a third check createBootstrapMarkerMigrationCheck if migration is surfaced as a separate fixable_error (recommended for explicit operator visibility; alternative bundles it into L2 drift). 2) runDoctorFix dispatcher (doctor.ts L893-L987) — add three new if-blocks, ideally in this order: marker migration FIRST (so L1/L2 detectors after see the new marker name), L1 fix SECOND (rewrite .fabric/AGENTS.md from canonical), L2 fix THIRD (re-run addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection equivalent on the three SECTION_TARGETS). All three call atomicWriteText. 3) TARGET_FILE_PATHS (doctor.ts L696-L702) — append '.fabric/AGENTS.md' and '.fabric/project-rules.md' so summary.targetFiles flags their presence in --json output. 4) Event ledger schema (packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts) — add z.literal('bootstrap_marker_migrated') and optional z.literal('bootstrap_l1_repaired') / z.literal('bootstrap_l2_repaired') for fix audit trail; the existing meta_reconciled / mcp_config_migrated patterns are templates. 5) emitDoctorRunEventBestEffort (cli/src/commands/doctor.ts L301-L316) — unchanged; doctor_run event continues to fire post-run with no new fields needed (issues count auto-includes the new checks). 6) Cross-package canonical-template access — see clarification_needs; this is the blocking design decision before TASK-N1 (inspect helpers) can be implemented.
constraints: 1) Byte-level diff — NO normalization layer. The task spec explicitly states 'no CRLF normalization; if write produces CRLF, that's an install-side bug'. Use Buffer.compare or === on string reads at default encoding; do not pre-process line endings. Add a test that asserts CRLF-on-disk triggers drift detection (regression guard against accidentally-added normalization). 2) Drift → abort, --fix overwrite — must NOT prompt or partial-fix; consistent with rc.15's --force removal under the existing 'drift→abort 不要 --force' DNA. The fixable_error kind already gives this semantics (operator must explicitly invoke --fix). 3) One-time migration constraint — the marker rename runs at most once per project root. After --fix flips the markers, the inspection must produce ok status on next run. Test the idempotency explicitly. 4) Atomic-write discipline — every rewrite path through atomicWriteText to avoid half-written-file states corrupting the next doctor run. 5) Best-effort observability — ledger event emission is best-effort (silent try/catch) per the established pattern; a ledger write failure must NOT roll back a successful file rewrite. 6) Cross-package boundary — server cannot import CLI internals. Move shared marker + template constants to packages/shared BEFORE writing the inspect helpers. 7) Migration window concern — the inspectL2ConcatDrift logic must recognize BOTH old (fabric:knowledge-base:*) AND new (fabric:bootstrap:*) markers during the same scan; otherwise a project that has not yet run --fix produces a confusing 'no managed block found' rather than 'old marker; migrate'. Recommendation: the marker-migration inspection finds the old marker → flags migration_required (fixable_error); the L2 drift inspection only runs when new markers are present (skipped with informational note otherwise).

clarification_needs.question: How should server-side doctor access the canonical Fabric package bootstrap template for L1 drift detection, given that packages/server has no dependency on packages/cli?
clarification_needs.context: rc.19 L1 drift compares `.fabric/AGENTS.md` against 'Fabric package canonical bootstrap template'. Today those template files live in packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ and packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template. The server package's package.json has only fabric-shared, MCP SDK, chokidar, express, minimatch, zod — no fabric-cli dep, no path-resolution from server to cli/templates/. We must choose how doctor reaches the canonical content.
options: Lift canonical bootstrap template content into packages/shared as exported string constants (or .ts module with the markdown body inline); cli install + server doctor both consume from shared
Keep templates as files in packages/cli/templates/; bundle a hash + copy into packages/shared at build time via a small build script so doctor reads from shared/dist/bootstrap-canon.ts

Keep templates in packages/cli; add a direct workspace dep server → cli (acceptable since CLI is a dev/install-time tool, but creates a cyclic-feel: cli depends on server for runDoctor*, server would depend on cli for templates)

clarification_needs.question: Should L1 drift compare the canonical template VERBATIM (with `{ projectName }` / `{ frameworkKind }` tokens preserved) or POST-INTERPOLATION (tokens substituted with current project values before byte-compare)?
clarification_needs.context: packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template contains '{ projectName }' (L1) and '{ frameworkKind }' (L19) interpolation placeholders. Install renders these at write-time. For drift detection, comparing rendered ↔ rendered tracks user-meaningful drift (the actual file content); comparing verbatim ↔ on-disk only works if install ALSO writes the template verbatim and a separate render step happens elsewhere. The CLAUDE.md bootstrap template (packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/CLAUDE.md) has no placeholders so it's verbatim-stable.
options: Render-then-compare: substitute project tokens in the canonical template, then byte-compare against .fabric/AGENTS.md (matches install behavior; user-meaningful drift)
Verbatim-compare with tokens preserved: install writes verbatim with tokens, doctor compares verbatim (simpler diff logic, but requires changing install to keep tokens unresolved)
Strip-and-compare: strip a known set of placeholder lines before compare on both sides (fragile)

clarification_needs.question: Should the marker migration (fabric:knowledge-base → fabric:bootstrap) surface as its own fixable_error code or be bundled into the L2 drift fix?
clarification_needs.context: Two designs both work: (a) separate fixable_error 'bootstrap_marker_migration_required' that runs FIRST under --fix and rewrites the marker tokens in place; subsequent L2 drift inspection on the SAME --fix run then sees new markers and compares content. (b) Bundled into L2 drift: a single 'bootstrap_l2_drift' code whose fix path detects old marker and rewrites tokens THEN content. Design (a) gives operators an explicit, granular audit trail (one ledger event per migrated project, clear pre/post diff). Design (b) reduces the number of new fixable_error codes from 3 to 2.
options: Separate fixable_error 'bootstrap_marker_migration_required' (explicit, granular audit; emits its own bootstrap_marker_migrated event)
Bundled into 'bootstrap_l2_drift' (fewer surfaces; marker rename is a side-effect of the L2 fix)
Marker migration runs automatically on EVERY --fix invocation as a pre-flight no-op when markers already new (no separate code; idempotent helper called by all fix paths)

_metadata.task_description: rc.19 bootstrap consolidation — extend fab doctor with two-layer drift detection (L1: Fabric package canonical bootstrap template ↔ .fabric/AGENTS.md; L2: .fabric/AGENTS.md + .fabric/project-rules.md concat ↔ three-end managed blocks) + marker rename migration logic (fabric:knowledge-base → fabric:bootstrap) under --fix only. Byte-level diff, drift→abort, --fix overwrite.
